diagrams.title: ISO 9126 Quality Model
diagrams.description: Hierarchical model showing six main quality characteristics and their sub-characteristics
elements: Functionality (Suitability, Accuracy, Interoperability, Security, Functionality compliance)
Usability (Understandability, Learnability, Operability, Attractiveness, Usability compliance)
Efficiency (Time behavior, Resource utilization, Efficiency compliance)
Maintainability (Analyzability, Changeability, Stability, Testability, Maintainability compliance)
Portability (Adaptability, Installability, Co-existence, Replaceability, Portability compliance)
diagrams.purpose: Understand what constitutes software quality and how to measure it

diagrams.title: Cost of Quality Pyramid
diagrams.description: Shows the three-tier cost structure of quality with failure costs being largest
diagrams.purpose: Visualize where quality budget should be focused - prevention over correction

diagrams.title: Testing Pyramid
diagrams.description: Shows distribution of testing efforts across different levels
elements: Top (smallest): System Testing and Acceptance Testing
diagrams.purpose: Understand testing strategy and effort distribution

diagrams.title: CMMI Maturity Levels
diagrams.description: Five-level staircase showing process maturity progression
diagrams.purpose: Understand organizational maturity in software processes

diagrams.title: Defect Life Cycle Flowchart
diagrams.description: Circular flow diagram showing states a defect goes through
elements: New → Assigned → Open → Fixed → Verified → Closed
diagrams.purpose: Track and manage defects through resolution

visuals.title: Importance Of Software Quality
visuals.description: Visual diagram for importance of software quality
